title: Reverse a String
description:
Read a string from input and print the **reversed version** of that string.

**Hint:** Python strings support slicing with a step value.

conceptLesson.title: String Slicing
conceptLesson.content: Python strings can be sliced with `[start:stop:step]`. A step of `-1` reverses the string. This is one of Python's most powerful features!
conceptLesson.code:
s = "Python"
print(s[0:3])    # 'Pyt' — first 3 chars
print(s[::-1])   # 'nohtyP' — reversed
print(s[1::2])   # 'yhn' — every 2nd char

hints: You can reverse a string with slicing: s[::-1]
Or use a loop to build the reversed string
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and print it reversed

title: Count Vowels
description:
Read a string and print how many **vowels** (a, e, i, o, u) it contains.

Count both uppercase and lowercase vowels.

conceptLesson.title: Iterating Over Strings
conceptLesson.content: You can loop through each character in a string with a `for` loop. The `in` operator checks if a character exists in another string. Use `.lower()` to handle case-insensitivity.

hints: Convert the string to lowercase first
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and count the vowels

title: Palindrome Check
description: Read a string and print **"Yes"** if it reads the same forwards and backwards (case-insensitive), otherwise print **"No"**.

conceptLesson.title: Palindromes & String Comparison
conceptLesson.content: A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards (like "racecar"). To check: convert to lowercase, reverse, and compare. Python makes this a one-liner!
conceptLesson.code:
s = "Racecar"
lowered = s.lower()           # "racecar"
reversed_s = lowered[::-1]    # "racecar"
print(lowered == reversed_s)  # True

hints: Compare the string with its reverse
starterCode:
# Write your code here
# Read a string and check if it's a palindrome
